summary: This is the first part of a comprehensive course on Zadul-Ma'ad (Provisions for the Hereafter), a classical Islamic text by Ibn al-Qayyim al-Jawziyyah. Sheikh Ali Mashhour begins by establishing the importance of understanding an author's background before studying their work. He provides an extensive biographical overview of Ibn al-Qayyim, detailing his full name (Muhammad ibn Abu Bakr), his lineage, birthdate in 691 AH, and his formative education in Damascus. The lecture emphasizes Ibn al-Qayyim's most influential teachers: his father Abu Bakr, Sheikh al-Islam Ibn Taymiyyah (with whom he spent 16 years), and Imam al-Mizzi, the foremost hadith scholar of his era. The speaker also mentions some of Ibn al-Qayyim's notable students, including Ibn Kathir and Ibn Rajab al-Hanbali, illustrating the interconnected chain of Islamic scholarship.
suggested_title: Introduction to Zadul-Ma'ad: The Life and Teachers of Ibn al-Qayyim al-Jawziyyah - Part 1
